channel.title: NPR Topics: Culture
channel.description: Art and entertainment commentary plus interviews, book reviews, movie reviews, music reviews, comedy, and visual art. Subscribe to podcasts and follow trends in music, painting, art, architecture, photography, and design.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Shrey Parikh, 14, wins the Scripps Spelling Bee after a nail-biting 'spell-off'
item.description: This is the 101st year of the national spelling competition, and the third time it's been decided by a rapid-fire "spell-off" since the practice was introduced in 2021
item.content:encoded:
This is the 101st year of the national spelling competition, and the third time it's been decided by a rapid-fire "spell-off" since the practice was introduced in 2021

(Image credit: Allison Robbert)
item.dc:creator: Rachel Treisman

item.title: Three high school students on the 'small true things' that matter in their lives
item.description: What's something that's important to know about you? Independent producer Samantha Broun interviews high school students about the small true things in their lives.
item.content:encoded: What's something that's important to know about you? Independent producer Samantha Broun interviews high school students about the small true things in their lives.

item.title: 'Look to your elders': Alfre Woodard shares her secret to Hollywood longevity
item.description: Woodard says not giving up is the key to her long career. In the Netflix series The Boroughs, she plays a resident of a retirement community where something supernatural is preying on the residents.
item.content:encoded: Woodard says not giving up is the key to her long career. In the Netflix series The Boroughs, she plays a resident of a retirement community where something supernatural is preying on the residents.
item.dc:creator: Tonya Mosley

item.title: 'American Rambler' traces the path, and truth-squads the tale, of Johnny Appleseed
item.description: Isaac Fitzgerald, author of Dirtbag, Massachusetts, grew up with tales of local townie Johnny Appleseed. So when he found himself in need of a long, mind-clearing walk, he traced the legend's path.
item.content:encoded: Isaac Fitzgerald, author of Dirtbag, Massachusetts, grew up with tales of local townie Johnny Appleseed. So when he found himself in need of a long, mind-clearing walk, he traced the legend's path.
item.dc:creator: Richard Horan

item.title: Trick or spice? How the 'nutmeg' came to dominate the soccer world
item.description: Nutmeg commonly spices up a holiday season eggnog. It's also one of the most sought-after trick moves in soccer.
item.content:encoded:
Nutmeg commonly spices up a holiday season eggnog. It's also one of the most sought-after trick moves in soccer.

(Image credit: Sarah Stier)
item.dc:creator: Ava Berger

item.title: Dancer Jenn Freeman reframes her art after late autism diagnosis
item.description: The documentary "Room to Move" follows choreographer and performer Jenn Freeman as she reframes her creative process to create an evening-length solo performance after an autism diagnosis at age 33.
item.content:encoded:
The documentary "Room to Move" follows choreographer and performer Jenn Freeman as she reframes her creative process to create an evening-length solo performance after an autism diagnosis at age 33.

(Image credit: Alexander Hammer)
item.dc:creator: A Martínez

item.title: Former Obama adviser reflects on the 'Battle for American Identity'
item.description: Ben Rhodes was a speechwriter and security adviser for President Obama. His book, All We Say, is a collection of 15 speeches — from Ben Franklin to Trump — about what it means to be American.
item.content:encoded: Ben Rhodes was a speechwriter and security adviser for President Obama. His book, All We Say, is a collection of 15 speeches — from Ben Franklin to Trump — about what it means to be American.
item.dc:creator: Terry Gross

item.title: The movie 'Pressure' leans into the drama of high-stakes weather forecasts
item.description: The new movie tells a story about how good meteorology can literally win wars. It also takes us back in time, to when the United States was at a disadvantage when it came to weather science.
item.content:encoded:
The new movie tells a story about how good meteorology can literally win wars. It also takes us back in time, to when the United States was at a disadvantage when it came to weather science.

(Image credit: Alex Bailey)
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Hersher

item.title: Conservative Christians love this painting of George Washington. The event it depicts may not have happened
item.description: A painting of George Washington is being used by the Trump administration to argue the founders were devout Christians, but historians have doubts about whether the moment depicted actually happened.
item.content:encoded:
A painting of George Washington is being used by the Trump administration to argue the founders were devout Christians, but historians have doubts about whether the moment depicted actually happened.

(Image credit: First Freedom Art Company)
item.dc:creator: Neda Ulaby

item.title: Dana White says it's an 'honor' to build a fight arena on the White House lawn
item.description: NPR's Steve Inskeep speaks with Dana White, president and CEO of the Ultimate Fighting Championship, about his plans to build a fighting arena on the White House lawn.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Steve Inskeep speaks with Dana White, president and CEO of the Ultimate Fighting Championship, about his plans to build a fighting arena on the White House lawn.
item.dc:creator: Steve Inskeep
